scores.comment: 120s idle watchdog and fallback chain are largely confirmed.
runtime field is right, proposed agent.model config path is not supported by schema.
Does not fully distinguish provider/model/agent timeout scope.
Misses schema validation and upstream proxy/stream timeout verification.
Directly adding agent.model.requestTimeoutMs is likely invalid or ineffective.

findings.description: Proposed agent.model.requestTimeoutMs is not accepted by current AgentModelSchema, which is a strict object with only primary and fallbacks.
evidence: ~/.npm-global/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/zod-schema.agent-runtime-BTWfpsXE.js:14-17: AgentModelSchema allows only primary and fallbacks and is strict.
findings.recommendation: Do not put requestTimeoutMs under agent.model. Use models.providers.<id>.timeoutSeconds: 600 or a dedicated reviewer provider.

findings.description: requestTimeoutMs is the runtime input to idle watchdog, but provider timeoutSeconds is the supported config path that becomes model.requestTimeoutMs.
evidence: model-Cp9whgWq.js:560-561 and 774-809 convert providerConfig.timeoutSeconds to requestTimeoutMs.
runtime-schema-BjT8eJxD.js:593 documents models.providers.*.timeoutSeconds raises LLM idle/stream watchdog ceiling.
findings.recommendation: Use provider timeoutSeconds or a copied dedicated provider for bd-eval-reviewer.

findings.description: Increasing idle timeout should reduce the observed 120s idle fallback but cannot eliminate all fallback/restart paths.
evidence: embedded-agent-L9tQiaO-.js:710-720 treats idle/timedOut as assistant timeout failures.
embedded-agent-L9tQiaO-.js:3870-3880 shows same-model idle retry is not allowed when fallbackConfigured is true.
findings.recommendation: State expected effect narrowly: eliminate/reduce this class of 120s idle timeout fallbacks; monitor other failover reasons separately.

findings.description: requestTimeoutMs is capped by explicit run timeout or agent default timeout when those are lower.
findings.recommendation: Ensure agents.defaults.timeoutSeconds and run-specific timeouts are above 600s.

findings.description: 600s is reasonable for large Claude/tool-payload cases but increases hang duration for genuinely stalled requests.
evidence: selection-BP0T9R9I.js:11160-11162: idle watchdog covers stream creation and iterator progress; successful next() resets timer.
findings.recommendation: Start with 600s for 1-3 cases; only increase to ~900s if 600s idle timeouts persist and upstream permits it.

findings.description: newapi provider/proxy/SDK transport may still impose their own request or stream timeouts.
evidence: openclaw.json:316-318 uses remote baseUrl https://new-api.mediportal.com.cn with anthropic-messages.
anthropic-CMNCM8N0.js:281-285 passes options.timeoutMs to SDK request timeout.
findings.recommendation: Collect model transport debug/provider gateway logs to identify whether future timeouts originate from OpenClaw, SDK, proxy, or upstream model.

direct_answers.q1: Runtime entry yes; proposed agent.model config path no. Use provider timeoutSeconds or dedicated provider.
direct_answers.q2: It should solve/reduce observed 120s idle fallback if model starts output within 600s; other fallback paths remain.
direct_answers.q3: No valid conflict with agent.model.requestTimeoutMs because that field is not accepted; provider timeoutSeconds becomes model.requestTimeoutMs.
direct_answers.q4: 600s is reasonable for grey rollout; ensure total run timeout is higher and monitor concurrency/wall time.
direct_answers.q5: Yes: newapi gateway, upstream provider, SDK, HTTP proxy/LB, and streaming idle behavior may still timeout independently.

recommended_fix: Create a dedicated reviewer provider copied from newapi-anthropic-vip with timeoutSeconds: 600 and point bd-eval-reviewer primary/fallbacks to it; alternatively set timeoutSeconds: 600 on newapi-anthropic-vip globally if the broader side effect is acceptable.
most_important_recommendation: Do not add requestTimeoutMs under bd-eval-reviewer.agent.model; set models.providers.<id>.timeoutSeconds: 600 (preferably on a reviewer-only provider).
